title: Anthropic Ships Claude 4.6 Sonnet with 200K Context
subtitle: The mid-tier model gets a context window expansion and improved instruction following
metaDescription: Anthropic releases Claude 4.6 Sonnet with 200K context window and improved instruction following.
heroImageAlt: Lightning refracting through tall glass panels above still water
bodyHtml:
Anthropic released Claude 4.6 Sonnet on Tuesday, extending the mid-tier model's context window from 128K to 200K tokens and claiming measurable improvements in instruction following and code generation. The model is available immediately through the Anthropic API and Amazon Bedrock.

The context expansion puts Sonnet on par with the company's Opus tier for document-heavy workflows — legal contract review, academic paper analysis, and multi-file codebase tasks where the previous 128K limit required chunking strategies that degraded coherence.

Benchmark gains were incremental rather than leapfrog: 3.2 points on MMLU, 4.1 on HumanEval, and 2.8 on GSM8K. Anthropic emphasized practical improvements over headline scores — specifically, a 15% reduction in instruction-following failures identified through red-teaming.
pullQuote: The context window expansion addresses the single most common request from enterprise customers — they want mid-tier economics with near-frontier capacity for document tasks.
whyItMatters: 200K context at Sonnet pricing changes the economics of document-heavy workflows. Companies that were forced to use Opus for long-context tasks can now run the same workloads at roughly 60% lower cost.
